preamble: The transformation of the voiced labiodental fricative /v/ to the voiced alveolar fricative /z/ is a documented sound change that involves a shift in the place of articulation. This change moves the point of constriction from the lips and teeth to the alveolar ridge, behind the upper front teeth, while maintaining voicing and the fricative manner of articulation.
sources: Mchomvu, M. M. (2022). Phonological Processes of Shambaa. International Journal of English Literature and Social Sciences (IJELS), 7(3).
